macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Cools More Than Expected in June, Reducing Fed Rate Hike Fears
items.summary: U.S. consumer inflation slowed more than expected in June, with lower gasoline prices driving the decline, leading traders to bet the Federal Reserve will wait to increase interest rates rather than act later this month.

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Reaffirms Commitment to Employment Mandate Amid Inflation Focus
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh stated on Tuesday he is equally committed to the Fed's employment mandate as his inflation mandate, which he has emphasized since taking over the central bank.

items.title: IBM Shares Plummet 24% After Q2 Revenue Forecast Misses Estimates
items.summary: IBM sparked a market rout on Tuesday after forecasting second-quarter revenue below estimates and signaling businesses are prioritizing data-center infrastructure spending over software, highlighting AI's toll on the sector.

items.title: Citigroup Beats Q2 Earnings Expectations with 45% Profit Surge
items.summary: Citigroup reported second-quarter net income of $5.83 billion, a 45% increase from the prior year, with per-share earnings of $3.15 surpassing analyst estimates of $2.74.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge to $87 Per Barrel Amid Escalating U.S.-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Brent crude climbed 4.5% to around $87 a barrel after the U.S. reimposed a naval blockade of Iran and both countries increased attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, raising fears of energy supply disruptions.

items.title: Indian Markets Fall as Sensex and Nifty Erase Rs 3 Lakh Crore in Wealth
items.summary: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty faced heavy selling pressure on Tuesday due to surging crude oil prices and renewed inflation concerns, with India's retail inflation rising to 4.38% in June.

items.title: HCL Tech Shares Drop Over 4% After Weaker Q1 Earnings and Muted Guidance
items.summary: HCL Tech delivered weaker-than-expected first-quarter numbers, raising concerns about slowing global discretionary spending and AI-led disruption in India's IT sector.

items.title: China's Q2 Growth Expected to Cool as Demand Slump Overshadows Exports
items.summary: China's economy likely cooled in the second quarter with growth drifting toward the lower end of Beijing's annual target, as an entrenched demand slump overshadowed resilient exports.

items.title: Foreign Investors Bought Net $132 Billion of U.S. Securities in May
items.summary: The Treasury Department reported that foreign investors purchased a net $132 billion of U.S. securities in May, according to the monthly Treasury International Capital report.

items.title: Egypt's Non-Oil Private Sector Contracts Sharply in June Amid Currency Issues
items.summary: Egypt's non-oil private sector contracted at its fastest pace in June 2026, with the PMI falling to 46, marking the sixth consecutive month below the 50 growth threshold.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $64,000 After US CPI Falls Below Expectations
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped 4.64% to $64,963 as June US CPI dropped 0.4% monthly, reducing rate hike odds and boosting risk-on sentiment across crypto markets.

items.title: Ethereum Leads $101M Short Liquidations Following Inflation Data
items.summary: Ethereum triggered over $56M in short liquidations as markets flipped green after cooler-than-expected inflation readings, with total network liquidations reaching $285M in 24 hours.

items.title: US-UK Taskforce Unveils Digital Asset Roadmap Promoting Stablecoin Innovation
items.summary: The US-UK transatlantic taskforce released a new roadmap focused on regulated stablecoins and tokenized finance, strengthening transatlantic digital asset coordination.

items.title: CFTC Orders Kalshi to Honor Michigan Trades Amid Regulatory Clash
items.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission intensified its dispute with Michigan by directing Kalshi to continue honoring trades involving state residents, escalating the regulatory conflict.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Privacy Team Spins Out as For-Profit EthSystems
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation's privacy team launched as for-profit EthSystems to serve institutions, backed by Vitalik Lubin and Bitmine, marking a major ecosystem development.

items.title: Ark Invest Adds $14M in Circle Shares While Selling Robinhood Position
items.summary: Ark Invest increased its Circle holdings by $14 million while exiting its Robinhood position, signaling shifting institutional sentiment toward stablecoin infrastructure.

items.title: Tether Leads $7M Pact Labs Funding Round for Digital Payroll Services
items.summary: Tether strengthened its digital payroll push by leading a $7 million Series A round for Pact Labs, expanding its enterprise payment infrastructure.

items.title: Ripple Joins x402 Foundation as Premier Member for AI Payments
items.summary: Ripple advanced its AI payments strategy by joining the x402 Foundation as a Premier Member, integrating blockchain with artificial intelligence payment systems.

items.title: Fidelity Launches Stablecoin FIDD on Crypto Exchanges
items.summary: Fidelity is launching its own stablecoin, FIDD, on its platform and major crypto exchanges, marking a significant entry of traditional finance into stablecoin issuance.

items.title: Crypto Investment Funds Record $281.8M Net Inflows After 8-Week Outflow Streak
items.summary: Crypto funds saw $281.8M in net inflows last week—$197.4M for Bitcoin and $84.4M for Ethereum—ending an 8-week streak of outflows and signaling institutional demand recovery.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Sells 2 Million Copies in First 24 Hours Amidst Resync Controversy
items.summary: Ubisoft's remastered title achieved massive sales immediately after launch, while the company's latest financial report removed a widely mocked claim regarding the game's development following public backlash.

items.title: Donkey Kong 64 PC Port Announced with High Frame Rates, Mod Support, and Widescreen
items.summary: A new PC version of the classic N64 game has been officially announced, featuring modern enhancements including high frame rates, full mod support, and widescreen compatibility.

items.title: Agent 64: Spies Never Die Coming to PC Next Month
items.summary: The GoldenEye and Perfect Dark-inspired stealth action game Agent 64: Spies Never Die has been confirmed for a PC release next month.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 and Warzone Season 5 Launches July 23
items.summary: Full details for the upcoming Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 and Warzone Season 5 have been revealed, with both titles set to launch on July 23.

items.title: Xbox Officially Reveals Brand-New Hardware Available for Pre-Order
items.summary: Xbox has officially unveiled new hardware, which is now available for pre-order, marking a significant update to the console lineup.

items.title: PlayStation Plus Premium July 2026 Game Revealed Early
items.summary: The specific game included in the PlayStation Plus Premium lineup for July 2026 has been revealed ahead of the official schedule.

items.title: Online Gaming Firms in India Move Supreme Court to Review 28% GST Verdict
items.summary: Indian online gaming companies have filed review petitions with the Supreme Court challenging the recent judgment upholding the 28% GST on the sector and retrospective tax demands exceeding Rs 1.5 lakh crore.

items.title: Ex-Xbox Studio Compulsion Games Goes Independent and Invites Partners
items.summary: Compulsion Games, the studio behind South of Midnight, has gone independent after leaving Xbox and is now inviting game and entertainment studios to collaborate on future projects.

items.title: Palworld Sees Huge Surge in Players After Version 1.0 Release
items.summary: Palworld hit a peak of over 855,000 concurrent players this weekend following the release of Version 1.0, marking its highest activity since its Early Access launch.

items.title: EA Sports FC 27 Cover Star Leak Hints at Familiar Face's Return After 8 Years
items.summary: A leak regarding the cover star for EA Sports FC 27 suggests the return of a well-known footballer who has not been featured on the cover for eight years.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft in bombshell lawsuit over AI gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI and two former Apple employees of stealing trade secrets to develop upcoming AI hardware, sending shockwaves through Silicon Valley.

items.title: OpenAI's first hardware device will be a screen-free smart speaker
items.summary: Bloomberg reports that OpenAI will launch a portable, screen-free smart speaker as its first consumer hardware product, days after Apple's lawsuit.

items.title: IBM shares plunge 25% as AI infrastructure spending hurts software revenue
items.summary: IBM sparked a market rout after forecasting Q2 revenue below estimates, signaling businesses are prioritizing data-center infrastructure over software spending.

items.title: New York becomes first U.S. state to ban new large-scale AI data centers
items.summary: Governor Kathy Hochul signed an executive order imposing the country's first statewide moratorium on hyperscale data centers to protect the environment and energy grid.

items.title: Meta plans $9.1 billion investment for first AI data center in Canada
items.summary: Meta announced it will invest over 13 billion Canadian dollars to build its first AI data center in Canada, which will be its largest outside the U.S.

items.title: Apple hikes MacBook and iPad prices due to AI-driven chip cost increases
items.summary: Apple raised prices on popular products including the MacBook and iPad because of rising memory and storage chip costs sparked by the AI boom.

items.title: U.S. lifts export controls on Anthropic's advanced Mythos AI model
items.summary: The White House removed export controls on Anthropic's Mythos AI model, allowing its release to select companies after ordering an export block earlier in the month.

items.title: TYLSemi raises $43 million to build custom AI chip components
items.summary: Startup TYLSemi, founded by executives from Qualcomm's recent AlphaWave acquisition, secured $43 million in early funding to help companies build their own AI chips.

items.title: European Union orders Meta to change 'addictive' Facebook and Instagram features
items.summary: EU regulators told Meta to make significant changes to addictive features on Facebook and Instagram or face steep fines for breaching social media laws.

items.title: Google releases Android 17 security bulletin to fix multiple vulnerabilities
items.summary: Google published the Android Security Bulletin with Android 17 Security Release Notes to address multiple security vulnerabilities in the operating system.
